text.title: Definition of Done — Multi-Role Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the definition-of-done-multi-role methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Every active role MUST appear as a row with ≥3 binary done-criteria; no role omitted.
rule.rationale: Missing rows silently shift work to whoever notices last.

rule.statement: Each criterion MUST be checkable in <60s (file exists / link present / test green); no subjective phrasing.
rule.rationale: Subjective criteria become political and produce no gate effect.

rule.statement: Each row MUST have a named owner; '@team' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without named owner the checklist becomes optional.

rule.statement: Every checked criterion MUST link to evidence (PR, test report, doc URL).
rule.rationale: Unlinked checkmarks have zero audit value and erode trust.

rule.statement: DoD MUST be reviewed each retrospective; un-reviewed in 6+ retros → marked stale.
rule.rationale: Unreviewed DoDs encode obsolete process and block velocity.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
